ops.title: wire server: dispatch classes, trust floor, disconnect watching
ops.body:
# Dispatch architecture

Server dispatches LF-framed JSON over an already-bound listener it never binds or closes (beyond intake-stop). Three handler classes: control runs inline on the accepting goroutine; concurrent runs in a bounded worker pool (Workers + Backlog; past capacity → typed Rejected reply, a proven non-dispatch that is safe to retry); exclusive serializes on one shared mutex. Orthogonally, a non-empty tenant key serializes same-tenant requests without blocking other tenants (tenantGates: capacity-1 channel per key, reference-counted so idle keys drop out). The tenant gate is taken on the CONNECTION goroutine, before any pool worker, so a same-tenant burst blocks its own connections, not other tenants' workers. Registration happens before Run; the handler table is read-only once Run starts; reservedOps panic on registration so a consumer can never shadow a lifecycle op. Startup order: OpenStore, BootReconcile, StartRealtimePlanes, then accept; shutdown: stop intake, cancel serve context, settle admitted running AND queued handlers (admitted work always enqueues and drains, ctx cancellation included), then CloseResources (skipped only on the OpenStore-failure path).

# Trust floor (security invariant)

`trust` enforces the same-effective-UID floor on every connection BEFORE any stricter check, then runs the optional Trust callback. **The floor is never replaced by a non-nil callback — a callback augments it, never bypasses it.** The check runs after the frame read so a denial consumes the request and delivers the rejection instead of racing a close.

# Disconnect watching (watchDisconnect)

A goroutine polls the connection's read side while the handler runs and cancels the request context the moment the peer goes away. Detection is a short-read-deadline poke: a repeated one-byte read that times out while the peer is present and returns EOF/reset once it disconnects (a one-request-per-connection peer sends nothing more after its frame). The returned stop drains the watcher and clears the read deadline before the reply write; cancel doubles as the op-deadline cancel, so a fired deadline stops the watcher too.

# Grace rationale

requestGrace bounds the initial frame read so a client that connects but never sends cannot pin a connection goroutine past shutdown; writeGrace bounds a response write so a stuck write cannot stall shutdown; on shutdown the pre-frame read is unblocked explicitly, else an idle connection holds connWG until its read deadline. Server.Version stamps responses with the CONSUMER's version, never wire's own.
